user_query:
I need you to act as my product logic naysayer and stress-test a concept using your three-stage questioning process with all iron rules. Read input/proposal.txt for the concept and input/user_responses.jsonl for the exact user replies per round. Produce a single transcript file at output/transcript.jsonl that captures ONLY your side of the conversation plus the required stage transitions and the final closing statement.

Strict requirements for the transcript:
- General rules:
  - Zero praise anywhere. Do not offer plans, advice, or implementation details. Only ask killer questions in question rounds; use statements only in the two mandatory stage transitions and in the single closing statement.
  - Max 2 questions per round. Each question must end with a question mark.
  - Stage 1 questions must not name any specific competing products; use generic terms only.
  - Never combine stages in a single round. Follow the exact sequence and counts below.

- Sequence and counts (exact):
  1) Stage 1 (Foundation): exactly 3 question rounds.
     - Use input/user_responses.jsonl lines with stage=1 and round=1..3 as the user’s replies driving your next question.
     - After the 3rd question round, insert a Stage 1 → Stage 2 transition.
       - In this transition, quote the exact user_reply from stage=1, round=2 as user_quote.
  2) Stage 2 (Evidence Interrogation): exactly 4 question rounds, one per dimension in this strict order: source, scale, match, direction.
     - Use input/user_responses.jsonl lines with stage=2 and round=1..4 as the user’s replies.
     - Each Stage 2 question round must include a field "dimension" with one of: "source", "scale", "match", "direction".
     - After the 4th Stage 2 round, insert a Stage 2 → Stage 3 transition.
       - In this transition, quote the exact user_reply from stage=2, round=3 as user_quote.
  3) Stage 3 (Pruning): exactly 2 question rounds.
     - Round 1 purpose: "irreducible_core" (ask the one-hour-ship minimum question).
     - Round 2 purpose: "side_effect" (challenge side-effects of the simplified core).
     - Use input/user_responses.jsonl lines with stage=3 and round=1..2 as the user’s replies.
  4) Closing Statement: exactly one, after Stage 3 Round 2.

- Output format: Write one JSON object per line (JSONL) to output/transcript.jsonl using ONLY the following schemas, in this exact order of steps:
  - Question round (Stages 1/2/3):
    {"type":"question","stage":<1|2|3>,"round":<integer>,"questions":["...", "..."][,"dimension":"source|scale|match|direction" (Stage 2 only)][,"purpose":"irreducible_core|side_effect" (Stage 3 only)]}
  - Stage transition:
    {"type":"transition","from_stage":<1|2>,"to_stage":<2|3>,"user_quote":"<exact quote>","surviving_logic":"<one sentence>","testing":"<what the next stage tests>"}
  - Closing statement:
    {"type":"closing","transformation":"From <original framing> to <surviving core>.","core_logic":"<one sentence>","go_validate":"<specific validation target>"}

- Additional formatting constraints the validator will enforce:
  - Every question string must end with a question mark and there must be 1 or 2 questions per round only.
  - No praise words anywhere (e.g., great, awesome, nice, congrats, well done, love).
  - Stage 1 questions must not contain explicit competitor/product names like "Slack", "Teams", "Discord", "Notion", "Airbnb", "eBay".
  - For each transition, user_quote must match exactly the user_reply from the specified input line.
  - "surviving_logic" in transitions and "core_logic" in closing must be a single sentence.
  - The closing "transformation" must start with "From " and contain " to ".

Do not print anything else to the console. Only write the JSONL transcript to output/transcript.jsonl.
